description:
Follow-up split out of fz-ul4.27.5.4. The captured-slot half of VR.3.4 shipped, but the continuation's slot 0 (the "result" slot the callee writes via emit_return) stays FzValue. Making it raw requires a multi-callsite analysis the single-FnTypes-per-fn model doesn't support today.

## Why it can't trivially be raw

Each continuation has its own schema. A given callee may be referenced from multiple Call sites, each with a DIFFERENT cont fn and therefore potentially different result-slot kinds. emit_return is in the callee — it can't statically know which cont schema applies at runtime. So a uniform tagged FzValue at the result slot is the only sound choice today.

## Possible paths

1. Pin per-callee return kind across all call sites: walk the module, for each callee, intersect the result-slot kinds expected by every cont reached via that callee. If all conts agree (e.g. all expect RawF64), set the callee's "return kind" to that and have emit_return store raw bytes; force every cont's slot 0 to match. If they disagree, fall back to FzValue. Requires ModuleTypes-level book-keeping.

2. Encode the cont's result-slot kind in the cont frame header (a runtime tag): emit_return reads it and dispatches. Adds a per-return branch.

3. Per-fn calling-convention specialisation (overlaps with VR.4 / fz-ul4.27.6): if a fn has a typed return, give it a per-fn ABI that returns the raw value directly. Continuations adapt at the call boundary.

## Acceptance

- Result slot of continuations whose callees have a single typed return type lowers to raw f64 / raw i64.
- The dist fixture's callsite (dist → print's continuation) emits a single raw f64 write at emit_return; no fz_alloc_float at the return boundary.
- Matrix green.

## Blocks

- Nothing in the .27.5.* chain — the captured-slot half (.5.4 main) is sufficient for VR.3.5 (closures). This ticket is a quality-of-implementation polish that can land anytime after VR.4.

comments.text: Closed as superseded by fz-ul4.29. The multi-callsite agreement problem that motivated this ticket is an artifact of the single-FnTypes-per-fn model. Under per-callsite specialization (fz-ul4.29), each cont is itself per-callsite-specialized; the cont's slot 0 kind equals the callee's specialized return kind by definition, with no agreement check needed. No work shipped from this ticket lands; the infrastructure I drafted (final_return_kinds fixed point, emit_return signature split) was reverted in full and is explicitly NOT carried forward — .29 makes it unnecessary.

title: VR.3.4 follow-up: typed result slot in continuation frames
